queue.task: Run initial full-site audit to establish baseline
queue.details: First run of the autonomous system — scan all 68 HTML files against audit.md checklist. Establish baseline health score and populate queue with discovered issues.
queue.completedNotes: Baseline audit complete. Site health: 92/100. Tier 1 all passing. 5 pages missing schema (areas hub, guides hub, quote, privacy, terms). All 68 content pages have canonical, meta desc, H1, OG tags. 0 clean URL violations. 27/27 guides have Article datePublished+dateModified.

queue.task: Verify sitemap completeness — all HTML pages accounted for
queue.details: Cross-reference all HTML files against sitemap.xml.
queue.completedNotes: Sitemap has 68 entries covering all content pages. Non-indexable files (logo-mockup.html, google verification) correctly excluded. All URLs use clean format.

queue.task: Verify all internal links use clean URLs (no .html extensions)
queue.details: Scan all HTML files for href attributes containing .html.
queue.completedNotes: 0 violations found across all 68 content files. All href attributes use clean URLs. Sitemap and canonical tags also clean.

queue.task: Update all sitemap lastmod dates to reflect actual file modifications
queue.details: Current sitemap lastmod dates are from Feb 2026. Check actual file modification dates and update sitemap accordingly.
queue.completedNotes: Updated 48 lastmod entries from 2026-02-19 to 2026-02-21 (actual git date from visual redesign commit). 8 entries at 2026-02-22 were already correct (Phase 5 files). All dates now match git history.

queue.task: Verify all Article schema has datePublished and dateModified
queue.details: Check all guide pages for Article schema with datePublished and dateModified properties.
queue.completedNotes: All 27 guide pages have Article schema with both datePublished and dateModified. 5 guides have datePublished of 2025-12-19 (accurate original publish date).

queue.task: Update stale year references from 2025 to 2026

queue.task: Verify all pages have complete OG meta tags

queue.completedNotes: All 68 content pages have og:title confirmed. Phase 6 OG implementation verified intact.

queue.task: Check all images for alt text and dimensions
queue.details: Verify all img tags have alt, width, height. Hero images should have fetchpriority=high, below-fold should have loading=lazy.
queue.completedNotes: 20 images found across root pages only (service hero + cards + before/after). All 20 fully compliant: alt text, width/height, fetchpriority=high on heroes, loading=lazy on below-fold. Area pages (22) and guide pages (27) have zero images. Validated by independent cross-check.

queue.task: Verify heading hierarchy on all pages (one H1, no skipped levels)
queue.details: Every page should have exactly one H1 containing primary keyword. No skipped heading levels (H1→H3 without H2). No duplicate H1s across site.
queue.completedNotes: Audit complete. PASS: All 66 pages have exactly 1 unique H1, no duplicate H1s. ISSUE FOUND: Footer H4 headings skip levels (no H3 parent) on 21 pages — new task SEO-019 created. Scanners missed this; validator caught it on cross-check.

queue.task: Ensure BLUF (answer-first) format on all guide opening paragraphs
queue.details: Check first paragraph of each guide page. Should directly answer the search query in first 60 words. Phase 4 did this for 8 guides — verify and fix remaining.
queue.completedNotes: All 27 guides ALREADY BLUF compliant. 25 use 'The quick answer:' format, 2 use 'The honest answer:', all open with direct answers within 35-76 words. No changes needed. Phase 4 was more thorough than recorded — it fixed all guides, not just 8.

Schema coverage now 66/68 content pages. Remaining 3 without schema: quote.html, privacy.html, terms.html (SEO-018, P3).

Next priority: SEO-009 (heading hierarchy audit, all pages, ICE 252, needs team) — the highest remaining automated task.
Heading audit: all 66 pages have exactly 1 unique H1, no duplicates. Main content hierarchy is clean.
Validator caught footer H4 heading skips that both scanners missed — demonstrates value of independent validation.
Footer H4 issue affects 21 pages. Lower SEO impact (navigation, not content) but still a best-practice violation.
Fix approach: replace footer H4 with styled spans or restructure. Will need batched execution (3 files max per task).
Session summary: 6 runs completed. 11/19 tasks done. Site health improved from 92 to ~95/100. All P1 and P2 automated tasks complete.

Fix approach: replace

with

— semantically correct since footer labels are navigation, not content headings.
CSS strategy: added .footer-heading alongside existing .footer-col h4 selector. This allows incremental batching without breaking styling on unfixed pages.
Batch 1 done (index.html + quote.html). 6 more batches created (SEO-020 through SEO-025, 3 files each, last batch has 2).
1 guide page (pressure-washing-vs-jet-washing.html) has content H4 tags for equipment types — these are legitimate content headings under H3, NOT a heading skip issue. No fix needed.
Future batches are higher ease (8 vs original 6) since CSS is done — just HTML find-and-replace.
Footer H4 batch 2 completed cleanly on 3 service pages. Pattern is fully proven and repeatable.
Service pages have non-footer H4 tags in FAQ and Related Services sections — these are correct content headings (under H3) and must NOT be changed.
The replace_all approach works perfectly for this — each footer H4 text (Services, Service Areas, Information, Contact) is unique enough to safely target.
5/19 pages now fixed. 5 more batches remaining (SEO-021 through SEO-025). At 1 batch per run, heading fix will complete in 5 more cycles.
Batch 3 complete — all 3 service pages (render, decking, commercial) fixed. 8/19 total pages done.
Validator timing issue: ran before editor finished, got false FAIL. Re-check after editor completed confirmed PASS. Lesson: validator should check TaskList before running.
All 6 service pages now have clean footer heading hierarchy. Remaining: 3 utility pages (calculator, pricing, faq), 3 info pages (privacy, terms, areas hub), 2 content pages (guides hub, pillar).
Pattern fully battle-tested across 8 pages now. Zero issues with the replace_all approach.
Batch 4 (roof, gutter, soft-washing) was completed by team in previous session but tracker wasn't updated. Confirmed edits in place and updated tracker.
11/19 pages done after this run.
FOOTER H4 FIX 100% COMPLETE — all 19/19 pages fixed in one accelerated run (batches 5-7 combined).

Discovery: 3 pages (privacy, terms, areas hub) and guides hub use 'Key Areas' instead of 'Service Areas' as footer heading. The footer structure is NOT identical across all root pages — two variants exist.

Pattern proved safe for batch execution: after 11 pages with zero issues, remaining 8 were done solo without team overhead. Safe threshold for repetitive find-and-replace: 10+ successful prior executions.

76 total footer-heading instances across 19 pages (4 per page). CSS selector .footer-heading works alongside legacy .footer-col h4 for any pages missed.

Team pattern continues to work well: editor + validator with blocked dependency. Validator waited correctly this time (no timing issue).
Session productivity: 12 runs, 19 tasks completed (14 in previous session, 5 this session). All P1/P2/P3 automated tasks except SEO-010 now done.
ALL AUTOMATED SEO TASKS COMPLETE. 20/20 automated tasks done. 0 remaining.

Guide opening paragraphs range 35-76 words, all within BLUF target of ~60 words. Strong featured snippet optimization across the board.

The autonomous SEO system has exhausted all automatable work. Next run should trigger a discovery scan for new issues, or the system is idle until manual tasks are done and new opportunities emerge.

P1 schema date typo in block-paving-sealing-guide.html: datePublished was '2026-12-19' (future date). Fixed immediately to '2025-12-19'. This was missed by SEO-006 (year freshness check) because it checked for stale '2025' refs, not wrong '2026' dates.
Heading hierarchy issues on index.html (H4 under H2 in showcase section) and calculator.html (H3 before H2). These are content headings, not footer headings — different fix from SEO-019 series.
29 pages have og:description not matching trimmed meta description. SEO-026 trimmed meta desc but didn't sync og:description. Lesson: when trimming meta descriptions, always sync og:description too.

Scanner flagged 23 pages with meta desc 156-170 chars, but reviewer correctly classified these as borderline (Google truncation varies 155-160). Not worth a separate task — the worst offenders were already fixed in SEO-026.
Near-duplicate area page titles are by design (template pattern for local SEO). Not an issue.
SEO-030+031 combined into one run — both single-file heading fixes, no team needed.
index.html: 3 showcase caption H4s → H3s. CSS updated to target both h4 and h3 in .showcase-item-caption for backward compatibility.
calculator.html: 2 form label H3s → H2s (promoted to correct level under H1). No CSS changes needed — no specific selectors existed.
